title: Questions from Quarantine: Protests, COVID-19, and Equality
summary: The Security and Global Health Project is proud to present a weekly web series with Security Mom Juliette Kayyem and Medicine Mom Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux. Each week, our experts will answer your questions from quarantine and give you advice on staying sane and sanitary in a global crisis. We hope you'll join our Moms every Tuesday, and if you have a question that you want answered, tweet with #QuestionsFromQuarantine.
body: The Security and Global Health Project is proud to present a weekly web series with Security Mom Juliette Kayyem and Medicine Mom Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux. Each week, our experts will answer your questions from quarantine and give you advice on staying sane and sanitary in a global crisis. We hope you'll join our Moms every Tuesday, and if you have a question that you want answered, tweet with #QuestionsFromQuarantine . _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The Security and Global Health Project hosts a weekly web series featuring experts Juliette Kayyem and Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux, who answer public questions submitted via social media about managing life during the COVID-19 pandemic. The series, titled 'Questions from Quarantine,' specifically addresses topics such as public protests during the pandemic and issues of equality exacerbated by the crisis. The article promotes viewer engagement by encouraging the submission of questions through Twitter. It positions the series as a resource for practical advice on maintaining mental and physical health during a global health emergency. The broader context includes references to other related expert analyses on pandemic response, school reopenings, migration crises, and the importance of sustained investment in mRNA vaccine research, highlighting the intersection of public health, security, and policy.
result.main_content_zh: 安全与全球健康项目推出了每周网络系列节目，由专家朱丽叶·凯耶姆和玛格丽特·布尔多博士主持，回答公众通过社交媒体提交的关于在COVID-19大流行期间管理生活的相关问题。该系列名为“隔离问题”，特别探讨了疫情期间的公众抗议活动以及危机加剧的平等问题。文章通过鼓励通过Twitter提交问题来促进观众参与，并将该系列定位为在全球卫生紧急情况下保持身心健康的实用建议资源。更广泛的背景包括对其他相关专家分析的引用，涉及疫情应对、学校重新开放、移民危机以及持续投资mRNA疫苗研究的重要性，突出了公共卫生、安全和政策的交叉点。
